name: Deep Link Crawler
description: Second-pass link extraction from the working pool. Reads URLs discovered by sibling Step 1 modules (sitemap-parser, page-links, browser-crawler), fetches those pages, and extracts links one level deeper. Useful for uncovering blog posts, news articles, and subpages not in sitemaps or navigation.
usage_notes: Reads from the working pool built by earlier Step 1 modules. Run after sitemap-parser and page-links. Only useful when earlier modules found pages containing deeper links (blogs, news sections).

options.name: max_pages_per_entity
options.label: Maximum pages to crawl per company
options.description: How many pages from the working pool to fetch and extract links from.

options.name: max_urls_per_page
options.label: Maximum URLs per crawled page
options.description: Limit how many new URLs to extract from each crawled page.

options.name: crawl_patterns
options.label: URL path patterns to crawl
options.description: One pattern per line. Only crawl pages whose URL path contains one of these strings. Leave empty to crawl all pool URLs (recommended — let Step 2 handle filtering).

options.name: same_domain_only
options.label: Same domain only
options.description: Only include discovered links pointing to the same domain as the company homepage.

options.name: exclude_already_discovered
options.label: Exclude already discovered URLs
options.description: Skip URLs that already exist in the working pool. Disabled by default — url-dedup in Step 2 handles deduplication better.

output_schema.found_on: string (URL of the page this link was discovered on)
